name: QuasAr6b
description: Archon1 variant evolved through video-based pooled screening with improved speed.

spectrum.name: QuasAr6b
spectrum.note: QuasAr6b is an Archon1-derived far-red Arch sensor. The Tian 2023 origin paper shows only phototag/photoactivation spectra, not a clean fluorescence excitation/emission spectrum; the displayed curve is the QuasAr1/QuasAr2 far-red fluorescence spectrum (ex 590 / em 715 nm; Hochbaum 2014, Suppl. Fig. 2d) inherited as the closest measured Arch-family spectrum.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean from biexponential fit at 30°C. Activation: 87% fast tau 0.8 ms, 13% slow tau 6.6 ms. Deactivation: 87% fast tau 0.8 ms, 13% slow tau 6.0 ms.

dynamicRangeData.note: In HEK293T cells, voltage clamp with 635 nm one-photon excitation, per 100 mV step, n=4 cells

sensitivityData.note: Per-spike ΔF/F from high-throughput Optopatch assay in cultured rat hippocampal neurons at room temperature, 635 nm one-photon excitation, highest virus titer, n=596 cells; visually estimated from Fig. 3i, with titer series in Ext. Fig. 5c. The 27 ± 3% value in Fig. 3c is a -70 to +20 mV voltage-clamp measurement, not AP sensitivity.

photostabilityData.note: Photobleaching by 635 nm laser at 420 W/cm² (= 4200 mW/mm²) over 10 min in HEK293T cells, n=2 cells. Caption states all constructs showed <40% photobleaching over 10 min; QuasAr6b trace ends at ~62-65% remaining. (Main text mis-cites this as Ext. Fig. 6f, but the photobleach panel is Ext. Fig. 4f.)

photobleach.note: Photobleaching re-digitized from Ext. Fig. 4f (QuasAr6b-Citrine, purple/indigo trace), HEK293T, continuous 635-nm laser at 420 W/cm² (= 4200 mW/mm²), 10 min, n=2 cells. Mono-exponential fit (a=1.01, tau=1330 s, r2=0.96), t75≈405 s (data interp 412 s). Measured alongside Archon1 (cyan) and QuasAr6a (red) — the purple/red traces nearly overlap and end ~62% at 10 min; Archon1 ends ~68%. (Prior stored curve traced ~9% too high, ending 71% with t75 503 s — corrected here to the true purple trace ending ~63% at 585 s. Consistent with photostabilityData ~62-65%.)

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, hippocampal PV interneurons, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Optical spike full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of optogenetically-triggered spikes in somQuasAr6b+ parvalbumin (PV) neurons, imaged at 2 kHz (1973 Hz), 635 nm one-photon, n=20 cells / 3 animals; 0.87 ± 0.11 ms (mean ± s.d.). Narrower than Archon1 (1.1 ± 0.15 ms). Distinct from the Fig. 3h/Ext.Fig.5b cultured-neuron 'spike width' (9.5 ms) which is defined as the full width at 80% below the AP peak, not FWHM. At 4 kHz the same measurement falls to 0.71 ms (Ext. Fig. 8g), approaching the patch-clamp FWHM 0.49 ms.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex layer 1 NDNF interneurons, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Optical spike full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of optogenetically-triggered spikes in somQuasAr6b-Optopatch layer-1 NDNF interneurons imaged in vivo at a 1 kHz (996 Hz) frame rate, 635 nm one-photon, n=29 cells / 2 animals; 2.3 ± 0.3 ms (mean ± s.d.), not significantly different from somQuasAr6a (2.4 ± 0.4 ms) or somArchon1 (2.7 ± 0.5 ms). Panel d is explicitly the optical-spike FWHM (per Fig. 4d caption), so it is directly comparable to the PV-cell 0.87 ms entry; the NDNF value is larger because NDNF spikes are broader than fast-spiking PV spikes and imaging was at half the frame rate. Cross-measured within QuasAr6's characterization (same Tian et al. 2023 paper).

researchPapers.title: Video-based pooled screening yields improved far-red genetically encoded voltage indicators
researchPapers.authors: Tian H, Davis HC, Wong-Campos JD, Park P, Fan LZ, Gmeiner B, Begum S, Werley CA, Borja GB, Upadhyay H, Shah H, Jacques J, Qi Y, Parot V, Deisseroth K, Cohen AE
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro; Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro; Mouse cortex NDNF interneurons, in vivo; Mouse hippocampus PV interneurons, in vivo

researchPapers.title: All-optical electrophysiology reveals behavior-dependent dynamics of excitation and inhibition in the hippocampus
